action.reasoning: Observation: Training crashed with RuntimeError: expected scalar type Double but found Float in torch.bmm(adj_norm, x). Mean/Std printed correctly. Analysis: normalize_adj produces float64 tensor (numpy promotion in 1.0/sqrt and diag), while x is float32 from DataLoader; model/predict/dataset logic is sound, dense adj efficient for N=207, no PyG needed. Decision: Explicitly cast all to np.float32 + .float() in normalize_adj and predict.py adj calc, ensure model forward handles consistent dtypes, run 5 epochs, submit_eval on best.pt for first mae_60min baseline and time measurement.
